does_not_establish: That the simulation contains every attacker path.
That blocked paths remain blocked in production.
That the mitigations are operationally safe without engineering review.
That this is a penetration test.
establishes: The simulator combined the supplied static and configured evidence.
The displayed paths follow the recorded graph composition rules.
Each mitigation corresponds to identified findings.

findings.confidence_basis: Composed from one static finding and two configuration-derived findings. The weakest input is CONFIG_DERIVED and the composition is INFERRED, so this path is a modelled path, not an observed one.

evidence.detail: dataset worker -> Python execution (static, loader.py:14) -> AWS credential readable (configured, captured 2026-06-01) -> s3:GetObject on prod-data (configured, captured 2026-06-01)

limitations: No edge in this path was dynamically observed.
The credential and policy edges rest on a description captured 2026-06-01 and may be stale.
Composition does not establish that the path is traversable end to end.
This is a simulation, not a penetration test.

findings.confidence_basis: The upstream template-injection check did not complete over its intended scope, so this entry point can be neither confirmed nor excluded. The incompleteness propagates rather than being discharged here.
limitations: This path must not be rendered with the confidence of a fully evidenced path.
It must equally not be dropped from the report; a silently omitted incomplete path reads as an excluded path.

failed.reason: Upstream scanner finding is PARTIAL: this file was never analysed, so no entry-point conclusion can be drawn from it.

generic_advice: Review whether dataset-processing workloads need any standing cloud credentials at all, after the specific changes above are applied.

mitigations.evidence_basis: Statement 0 of the attached role policy, from an environment description captured 2026-06-01T00:00:00+00:00.
mitigations.expected_path_removed: dataset worker -> AWS credential -> s3:GetObject on prod-data
mitigations.proposed_change: Remove the s3:GetObject Allow on arn:aws:s3:::prod-data/* from dataset-worker-role, or scope it to the specific prefixes the loader requires.
mitigations.residual_risk: Other roles assumable by the worker were not supplied and were not modelled; removing this grant does not address them.
mitigations.trade_offs: Any legitimate loader path that reads prod-data will fail until the required prefixes are enumerated and re-granted.

mitigations.evidence_basis: The supplied NetworkPolicy permits the link-local range, contradicting the operator's assertion that metadata access is blocked (contradiction C-101).
mitigations.expected_path_removed: dataset worker -> cloud metadata endpoint -> instance role credentials
mitigations.proposed_change: Add an except clause for 169.254.169.254/32 (and fd00:ec2::254/128 where IPv6 egress is permitted) to the 0.0.0.0/0 egress ipBlock.
mitigations.residual_risk: A CNI-level block may already exist and was not described; if so this change is redundant rather than harmful.
mitigations.trade_offs: Workloads in this namespace that legitimately read instance metadata will break; those should use projected service-account tokens instead.

residual_uncertainty: This is a simulation over declared, configured, static and inferred evidence, two edges of which rest on a description captured 2026-06-01T00:00:00+00:00. One path is incomplete because an upstream check did not finish. Paths not modelled here are not thereby excluded.

reasoning_notes: Composed 1 fully-evidenced path and 1 incomplete path.
The incomplete path is retained and labelled; dropping it would read as an excluded path.
Two configuration-derived edges carry capture time 2026-06-01T00:00:00+00:00.

unknowns.reason: No edge in the path was dynamically observed.
unknowns.subject: Whether the modelled path is traversable end to end
unknowns.would_be_resolved_by: A sandboxed execution under a reviewed profile.
